_note: The Sumerian King List — a SEPARATE Mesopotamian tradition, deliberately NOT connected to Adam's line. Two honest reasons: (1) no documented bloodline links Sumerian/Akkadian kings to anyone else in this tree (dynasties were broken by conquest), and (2) the historical kings (Gilgamesh ~2700, Enmebaragesi ~2600, Sargon ~2334 BC) predate the biblical Ussher Flood date (~2348 BC), so they can't sit below Noah. Rendered as a SUCCESSION chain (a king-list), not a father-son bloodline — the header says so. Legendary at the top; historical from Enmebaragesi/Sargon down. All ids prefixed SUM_ so they classify to the SUMER house. This is its own root; the tree now has two components by design.

a.label:
Alulim
c.3300 BC · first king of Eridu; 'kingship descended from heaven' · legendary

children.label:
Alalngar
c.3270 BC · king of Eridu · legendary

En-men-lu-ana
c.3240 BC · king of Bad-tibira · legendary

En-men-gal-ana
c.3210 BC · king of Bad-tibira · legendary

Dumuzid the Shepherd
c.3180 BC · king of Bad-tibira; the shepherd-god · legendary

En-sipad-zid-ana
c.3150 BC · king of Larak · legendary

En-men-dur-ana (Enmeduranki)
c.3120 BC · 7th king, of Sippar; master of divination — often paralleled with Enoch, the 7th patriarch · legendary

Ubara-Tutu
c.3000 BC · last king before the flood, of Shuruppak · legendary

Ziusudra (Utnapishtim)
c.2950 BC · the flood-hero who survived the deluge — the Mesopotamian Noah · legendary

Etana of Kish
c.2850 BC · 'the shepherd who ascended to heaven' · legendary

Enmerkar of Uruk
c.2800 BC · built Uruk; hero of Sumerian epic · legendary

Lugalbanda of Uruk
c.2760 BC · epic hero; father of Gilgamesh in tradition · legendary

Gilgamesh of Uruk
c.2700 BC · hero of the Epic of Gilgamesh · legendary (possible historical kernel)

Enmebaragesi of Kish
c.2600 BC · earliest king with contemporary inscriptions — the first HISTORICAL figure of the list

Sargon of Akkad
c.2334 BC · founder of the Akkadian Empire — the world's first empire · historical

Naram-Sin of Akkad
c.2254 BC · Sargon's grandson; declared himself a god · historical

Ur-Nammu of Ur
c.2112 BC · founded the Third Dynasty of Ur; oldest surviving law code · historical

Shulgi of Ur
c.2094 BC · Ur-Nammu's son; the height of the Ur III empire · historical
